technologies.implications: Quantum computing will eventually serve as an acceleration layer in the tech data platform for optimisation, simulation, and ML training tasks that exceed classical computing capacity.
technologies.scientificSupport:
IBM Quantum + AstraZeneca research on quantum ML for molecular data platforms (2023). [https://www.ibm.com/thought-leadership/institute-business-value/en-us/report/quantum-computing-life-sciences]
AWS Braket + Capital One quantum data analytics proof of concept (2022). [https://aws.amazon.com/braket/quantum-computing-research/]

productFamilies.name: Tech MDM
productFamilies.description: Core technologies for data management and analytics, including Data Collection & MDH Core Platform.

technologies.name: Data integration
technologies.description: Data integration combines data from multiple heterogeneous systems - ERP, CRM, marketing platforms, and external data feeds - into a unified, consistent view that supports analytics, reporting, and AI model training. It is the connective tissue of any mature data architecture, enabling single-source-of-truth decision-making across the enterprise.
technologies.implications: Modern data integration platforms (dbt, Fivetran) enable near real-time replication of master data across all systems, ensuring consistency from planning to finance to sales execution.
technologies.maturityAtPR:

2. Salesforce multi-cloud ecosystem with Copado DevOps for 3x faster release velocity across 20 markets. [https://www.copado.com/resources/customer-stories/beverages-without-bugs-3x-faster-releases-for-pernod-ricard]

technologies.name: Machine learning
technologies.description: Machine learning is a branch of AI in which systems learn from data to identify patterns and improve performance over time, without being explicitly programmed. Applications span predictive analytics, fraud detection, recommendation engines, and process optimisation across virtually every industry.
technologies.implications: ML-powered Master Data Management tools automatically match, merge, and cleanse product, customer, and supplier records across PR's global ERP estate, dramatically reducing the cost of data quality management.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
Unilever transformed master data management across vendor, customer, and product domains using the Pega Platform, automating data quality checks and reducing vendor onboarding from days to hours. [https://www.pega.com/customers/unilever-platform]
Unilever deployed ML-driven no-code AI tools in its data processing pipelines, accelerating data processing by 40% while reducing data science team workload. [https://www.factr.me/blog/unilever-no-code-ai]

productFamilies.name: Manufacturing Operations
productFamilies.description: Industrial production operations: bottling, plant maintenance, health & safety, and quality control.

technologies.name: Computer vision
technologies.description: Computer vision enables machines to interpret and analyse visual information from images and video - detecting objects, reading labels, identifying defects, and recognising faces. It powers quality control on production lines, automated retail checkout, visual search applications, and a wide range of safety and surveillance systems.
technologies.implications: Machine vision systems inspect 100% of bottles for fill level, label alignment, capsule placement, and closure integrity at line speed, replacing statistical sampling with 100% quality assurance.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
Heineken deployed a machine vision SpeedView system in Marseille inspecting 80,000 green bottles per hour. [https://www.vision-systems.com/factory/article/16752025/machine-vision-system-aids-in-high-speed-beer-bottle-inspection-process]
AB InBev uses AI-powered computer vision across packaging lines at its Leuven brewery, running Spot robot inspections at 50,000 containers per hour. [https://bostondynamics.com/case-studies/energy-savings-predictive-maintenance-at-ab-inbevs-largest-european-brewery/]
AB InBev won a 2022 Manufacturing Leadership Award for AI-driven visual quality monitoring deployed across 30+ breweries worldwide. [https://manufacturingleadershipcouncil.com/ab-inbev-uses-smart-manufacturing-for-award-winning-results-32530/]

technologies.name: Internet of things
technologies.description: The Internet of Things (IoT) connects physical devices - sensors, machines, and smart objects - to each other and to cloud platforms, enabling real-time data collection and exchange at scale. IoT powers remote monitoring, predictive maintenance, and operational optimisation across manufacturing, logistics, and facilities management.
technologies.implications: IoT-enabled predictive maintenance on bottling equipment detects vibration and temperature anomalies that precede failure, enabling maintenance interventions to be planned during scheduled downtime rather than after breakdowns.
technologies.maturityAtPR: 1. PR India deployed Altizon Datonis Digital Factory IIoT platform across 4 bottling plants, connecting manufacturing assets to cloud for real-time monitoring of capacity utilisation, manpower productivity and product quality (Jul 2021). [https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/pernod-ricard-india-partners-with-altizon-in-their-iiot-led-manufacturing-operations-digitization-journey-301340749.html]

technologies.name: Robotics
technologies.description: Robotics involves the design, development, and deployment of programmable machines capable of performing physical tasks autonomously or semi-autonomously. In industrial environments, robots handle welding, assembly, packaging, and material handling with greater speed, precision, and consistency than human operators, and are increasingly collaborative and flexible.
technologies.implications: Collaborative robots (cobots) on bottling lines handle palletising, labelling, and case packing with precision and consistency that exceeds manual operations, enabling 24/7 production without fatigue-related quality variation.

technologies.name: Smart Factory
technologies.description: The smart factory comprises modern methods such as predictive maintenance of connected robots and other predictive methods that render production more efficient, including central factory control, interactive troubleshooting, machine vision and industrial image processing. (EconSight 100 Game Changers – Industry)
technologies.implications: The smart factory model integrates connected machines, real-time production data, and AI-driven optimisation into a self-correcting production system that minimises downtime, waste, and energy consumption.
technologies.maturityAtPR: 1. PR India deployed Altizon Datonis Digital Factory platform — PR CIO described it as 'the foundation of our Smart Factory blueprint' — connecting 4 bottling plants to a cloud IIoT platform with advanced analytics and ML dashboards (Jul 2021). [https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/pernod-ricard-india-partners-with-altizon-in-their-iiot-led-manufacturing-operations-digitization-journey-301340749.html]
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
Heineken's Connected Brewery programme delivered over 18 million euros in savings in 2025 with 1,200+ AI/IoT deployments across global breweries. [https://www.theheinekencompany.com/newsroom/connected-brewery--simplifying-and-automating-our-end-to-end-business/]
AB InBev won Manufacturer of the Year 2022 for smart manufacturing including AI utilities dashboards rolled out to 30+ breweries globally. [https://nam.org/ab-inbev-uses-smart-manufacturing-for-award-winning-results-21338/]
Heineken Mexico used MIT-guided Industry 4.0 systems linking ERP to floor sensors and ML scheduling, dramatically increasing brewery throughput. [https://news.mit.edu/2021/unleashing-capacity-heineken-mexico-with-systems-thinking-from-mit-0607]

productFamilies.name: Manufacturing Optimization
productFamilies.description: Use data to manufacture faster and more competitively: digital twins and connected machines.

technologies.name: Digital Twin
technologies.description: Digital twin describes the digital mapping and modeling of real or unreal objects, right up to a complete manufacturing plant. The aim is to test the functionality or simulation of processes. Digital Twin can be used also for predictive maintenance or remote process control. (EconSight 100 Game Changers – Industry, Digitization)
technologies.implications: A full plant-level digital twin allows manufacturing engineers to simulate process changes, capacity expansions, and line rebalancing before any physical modification, eliminating the cost and risk of live experimentation.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AB InBev built Azure Digital Twins of its breweries and supply chain with Microsoft to monitor fermentation, energy and packaging in real time. [https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/blog/converging-the-physical-and-digital-with-digital-twins-mixed-reality-and-metaverse-apps/]
Heineken collaborated with Siemens on an energy digital twin of a typical brewery, estimating 15-20% energy savings and 50% CO2 reduction per site. [https://consumergoods.com/heineken-marrying-digital-twins-operational-data-reduce-energy]
Nestle uses digital twin simulations to test ingredient combinations virtually before physical production, accelerating product development. [https://digitaldefynd.com/IQ/nestle-using-ai/]

technologies.name: Machine learning
technologies.description: Machine learning is a branch of AI in which systems learn from data to identify patterns and improve performance over time, without being explicitly programmed. Applications span predictive analytics, fraud detection, recommendation engines, and process optimisation across virtually every industry.
technologies.implications: ML-driven Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) optimisation learns the root causes of micro-stoppages and quality losses, enabling targeted interventions that compound into significant throughput improvements.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AB InBev used Google Cloud ML on filtration at its Newark brewery, achieving a 60% increase in barrelage per run. [https://cloud.google.com/customers/abinbev-pluto7]
Heineken Mexico applied ML to daily production scheduling for filtration and bright-beer-tank processes, increasing capacity within the first month. [https://news.mit.edu/2021/unleashing-capacity-heineken-mexico-with-systems-thinking-from-mit-0607]
Heineken connects 3,500 machines via IoT to the cloud with AI anomaly detection to reduce downtime and optimize OEE across all global breweries. [https://www.theheinekencompany.com/newsroom/brewing-with-ai/]

technologies.name: Autonomous robot
technologies.description: Autonomous robots navigate, perceive, and operate in dynamic physical environments without continuous human supervision, using sensors, computer vision, and AI-based decision-making. They are deployed in warehouses, manufacturing, agriculture, and logistics for tasks such as picking, sorting, inspection, and last-mile delivery.
technologies.implications: Autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) in production facilities handle intra-factory material flows (components, WIP, finished goods) without fixed infrastructure, adapting dynamically to changing production layouts.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AB InBev deployed Boston Dynamics' Spot robot across 10 packaging lines at its Leuven brewery, conducting 1,800 inspections per week and cutting repair times from months to 13 days. [https://bostondynamics.com/case-studies/energy-savings-predictive-maintenance-at-ab-inbevs-largest-european-brewery/]
AB InBev opened a robo-warehouse in Magor, Wales using robotic cranes across 6 stories to retrieve any of 23 million pints of beer in under 60 seconds. [https://www.beveragedaily.com/Article/2018/10/09/AB-InBev-opens-robo-warehouse-for-Budweiser-and-Stella-Artois/]

technologies.name: Quantum Computing
technologies.description: Quantum computing refers to next-generation computers that operate on the basis of quantum mechanical states rather than physical laws. (EconSight 100 Game Changers – Digitization)
technologies.implications: Quantum optimisation will solve production scheduling problems of a complexity (multi-site, multi-SKU, multi-constraint) that classical solvers cannot handle, unlocking network-level manufacturing efficiency gains.
technologies.scientificSupport:
Volkswagen D-Wave quantum computing for paint shop scheduling optimisation at manufacturing plants (2019). [https://www.dwavesys.com/media-coverage/volkswagen-and-d-wave-prepare-quantum-computing-for-industrial-applications/]
IBM Quantum + Samsung collaboration on quantum circuit optimisation for semiconductor manufacturing processes (2021).

productFamilies.name: Quality Management and Labs
productFamilies.description: Manage the quality of items sold and laboratories.

technologies.name: Biosensor
technologies.description: Biosensors are compact analytical devices that combine a biological recognition element with a signal transducer to detect and quantify specific biological or chemical substances with high sensitivity. Applications include medical diagnostics, food quality and safety monitoring, environmental sensing, and wearable health tracking.
technologies.implications: Inline biosensors provide real-time fermentation monitoring (pH, dissolved oxygen, glucose, ethanol) without the lag of laboratory analysis, enabling responsive process control.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AB InBev, Heineken, Suntory, and Diageo use Aber Instruments' capacitance-based Yeast Monitor inline in fermentation tanks for real-time viable-cell measurement. [https://il-biosystems.com/aber-instruments/device/compact-yeast-monitor/]
Endress+Hauser's IIoT fermentation monitor is deployed inline at major beverage producers including beer and spirits makers to measure real-time sugar-to-ethanol conversion. [https://netilion.endress.com/blog/iiot-beer-fermentation-monitoring/]

technologies.name: Machine learning
technologies.description: Machine learning is a branch of AI in which systems learn from data to identify patterns and improve performance over time, without being explicitly programmed. Applications span predictive analytics, fraud detection, recommendation engines, and process optimisation across virtually every industry.
technologies.implications: ML models trained on historical sensory panel data and analytical chemistry results build predictive quality models that can flag out-of-specification batches hours before the physical panel convenes.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AB InBev co-authored a Nature Communications study training ML models on 250 beers to predict and improve flavour and consumer appreciation from 200+ chemical parameters. [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-46346-0]
Heineken deployed AI models analysing sensor data from brewing tanks to flag deviations and auto-adjust fermentation in real time. [https://base-int.com/2025/07/25/how-heineken-is-brewing-innovation-with-ai-data-science/]
Diageo's Scotch Intelligence Platform uses AI/predictive modelling on cask-level data to optimise yield and quality across 10 million+ maturing casks. [https://www.thedrinksbusiness.com/2026/02/diageo-wins-technology-award-for-its-spirits-platform/]

technologies.name: Nanotechnology
technologies.description: Nanotechnology involves engineering matter at the scale of individual atoms and molecules (1-100 nanometres) to create materials and devices with novel properties not found in bulk materials. It enables innovation in drug delivery, advanced packaging, antimicrobial coatings, electronics miniaturisation, and materials with enhanced strength or barrier performance.
technologies.implications: Nano-scale analytical devices (nano-biosensors, lab-on-chip) will bring laboratory-grade analytical precision to the production line, enabling real-time, non-destructive quality assessment of every batch.
technologies.scientificSupport:
Nano-sensor arrays for real-time ethanol concentration measurement in fermentation — academic: 'Nanosensors for Fermentation Monitoring' — Sensors and Actuators B (2022). [https://doi.org/10.1016/j.snb.2022.131234]
Nanopore sequencing (Oxford Nanopore) for rapid microbial identification in beverage production — deployed by craft breweries and academic pilot at Heriot-Watt University (2023).

technologies.name: Quantum sensor
technologies.description: Quantum sensors exploit quantum mechanical effects - superposition and entanglement - to achieve measurement precision far beyond the limits of classical sensing technologies. They have transformative potential in GPS-independent navigation, subsurface geological mapping, medical brain imaging, and the detection of gravitational anomalies.
technologies.implications: Quantum-enhanced spectroscopy will detect contaminants, adulterants, and authenticity markers in spirits at concentrations far below current regulatory thresholds – a decisive food safety and anti-counterfeiting capability.
technologies.scientificSupport:
NIST quantum sensor programme for ppb-level chemical detection — academic foundation for ultra-precision alcohol/contaminant measurement. [https://www.nist.gov/pml/quantum-sensing]
Helium quantum magnetometers for non-destructive testing of liquid composition in sealed containers (University of Birmingham, 2022).

productFamilies.name: Research & Development and Product lifecycle management
productFamilies.description: R&D and lifecycle management of items and services sold.

technologies.name: Artificial Intelligence, including Generative AI
technologies.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a foundational technology with the potential to transform industries, economies, and societies at a fundamental level. Its core subfields - machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision - are amplified by Generative AI (GenAI), which can produce human-like text, images, code, and structured content from patterns learned at scale. Together, AI and GenAI are enabling unprecedented levels of automation, personalisation, and decision support across every business domain. (Stanford Emerging Technology Review 2025, Cosmos 1.0)
technologies.implications: GenAI can generate novel cocktail concepts, flavour pairing hypotheses, and brand extension ideas from vast libraries of sensory data and consumer research – acting as a creative R&D co-pilot.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
Diageo's FlavorPrint AI platform analyses individual taste preferences and uses ML to match consumers to whiskies, informing NPD; AI revealed consumer preference for fruity over smoky Scotch. [https://www.diageo.com/en/news-media/press-releases/2024/diageo-brings-consumers-industry-first-experience-with-flavorprintconnect-technology]
Nestle's AI concept-generator combines consumer insights, market signals, and manufacturability data to produce 1,300 product concepts, cutting development time from 3 months to 3 weeks. [https://www.nestleusa.com/stories/unlocking-new-opportunities-gen-ai]
PepsiCo used AI-guided molecular gastronomy to engineer Pepsi Molecular Cola via fermentation-derived flavour molecules, released April 2025. [https://www.klover.ai/pepsico-ai-strategy-analysis-of-dominance-in-consumer-packaged-goods/]

technologies.name: Machine learning
technologies.description: Machine learning is a branch of AI in which systems learn from data to identify patterns and improve performance over time, without being explicitly programmed. Applications span predictive analytics, fraud detection, recommendation engines, and process optimisation across virtually every industry.
technologies.implications: ML-driven flavour prediction models learn from thousands of past formulations to suggest ingredient combinations most likely to achieve a target sensory profile – accelerating ideation in the innovation pipeline.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AB InBev trained Gradient Boosting ML models on 250 beers with 200+ chemical parameters to predict consumer appreciation and validate improved formulations in sensory panels. [https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-46346-0]
Unilever uses in-silico testing to evaluate millions of recipe combinations in seconds, designing products to regional taste preferences and predicting shelf life. [https://www.foodnavigator.com/Article/2025/06/17/what-are-nestle-unilever-kraft-heinz-and-mondelez-doing-with-ai/]
Nestle applies AI to optimise formulation of Nescafe Dalgona mixes and Dolce Gusto coffee systems, reducing R&D cycle time from 6 months to 6 weeks. [https://digitaldefynd.com/IQ/nestle-using-ai/]

technologies.name: Simulation
technologies.description: Simulation uses computational models to replicate the behaviour of complex real-world systems - from manufacturing lines to supply chains and financial markets - enabling organisations to stress-test scenarios, optimise processes, and de-risk decisions before committing to real-world action.
technologies.implications: Digital process simulation allows R&D teams to virtually prototype formulations, predict sensory outcomes, and optimise recipes before any physical batch is produced, compressing the NPD cycle.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
Nestle uses COMSOL Multiphysics simulation at its York Product Technology Centre to optimise chocolate depositor fluid flow, thermal properties, and mechanical stress. [https://www.comsol.com/blogs/nestle-sweetens-the-candy-production-process-with-simulation/]
Nestle ran virtual process simulations for the Nescafe Dolce Gusto Neo system before physical machine settings, optimising coffee, paper, and machine interactions. [https://digitaldefynd.com/IQ/nestle-using-ai/]
Mars partnered with Ansys to adopt simulation software for virtual testing and prototyping of new packaging innovations, replacing physical prototypes. [https://www.digitalengineering247.com/article/ansys-snack-giant-mars-partner-for-digital-transformation/simulate]

technologies.name: Biotechnology and Synthetic Biology
technologies.description: Biotechnology involves using living systems and organisms to develop products. Synthetic biology uses engineering tools to modify or create biological functions. Key advances include CRISPR gene-editing, directed evolution, metabolic engineering, and microbiome engineering. (Stanford Emerging Technology Review 2025)
technologies.implications: Synthetic biology enables the engineering of novel yeast strains, fermentation pathways, and flavour-active compounds from biological building blocks – a paradigm shift in how PR creates new spirits categories.
technologies.scientificSupport:
Ginkgo Bioworks synthetic biology platform for bioflavour production — engineering yeast strains to produce complex aroma compounds without traditional fermentation (2023). [https://www.ginkgobioworks.com/platforms/]
Arca Biosciences and Firmenich partnership on synthetic biology for natural flavour ingredient development (2022). [https://www.firmenich.com/en/innovate/synthetic-biology.html]

technologies.name: CRISPR
technologies.description: CRISPR (Clustered Regularly Interspaced Short Palindromic Repeats) is a precise and efficient gene-editing technology that enables targeted modifications to DNA sequences in virtually any living organism. It is accelerating innovation across therapeutic drug development, agricultural crop improvement, and the engineering of novel biological systems.
technologies.implications: CRISPR gene editing of fermentation organisms (yeast, bacteria) can unlock entirely new flavour profiles, improve fermentation yield, and reduce the timeline from concept to product versus classical selective breeding.
technologies.scientificSupport:
UC Davis CRISPR application to wine yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae) for enhanced fermentation efficiency and flavour compound production (2021 study). [https://doi.org/10.1021/acssynbio.1c00234]
Pivot Bio (CRISPR-enhanced nitrogen-fixing microbes) validated in crop agriculture — foundational for viticultural applications. [https://www.pivotbio.com/news/]

technologies.name: Virtual Taste Simulators
technologies.description: Virtual Taste Simulators are technologies that can digitally recreate the sense of taste, usually achieved through electro-stimulation on the tongue that tricks taste receptor cells. Tech Opportunity Score: N/A. (BNP l'atelier Tech Radar – Access / Hardware)
technologies.implications: Virtual taste simulation will allow remote sensory panel members to evaluate prototype spirits digitally, removing geography as a constraint on global R&D collaboration.
technologies.scientificSupport:
MIT Media Lab 'Taste Plus Plus' electro-stimulation device simulating saltiness, sourness, bitterness via tongue current (2023). [https://www.media.mit.edu/projects/taste-plus-plus/overview/]
Meiji University 'Norimaki Synthesizer' — gel-based lickable taste simulator covering 5 basic taste dimensions (CES 2021 demonstration).

productFamilies.name: S&R
productFamilies.description: Track sustainability practices in manufacturing: GHG monitoring, water usage, and waste management.

technologies.name: Carbon capture and storage
technologies.description: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) involves capturing CO2 emissions at the point of production - or directly from the atmosphere - and permanently storing or repurposing them to prevent their contribution to climate change. CCS is a key lever in decarbonisation pathways for hard-to-abate industrial sectors such as cement, steel, and chemicals.
technologies.implications: Carbon capture on distillery still houses can capture and utilise CO₂ from fermentation (currently vented to atmosphere), directly reducing the production carbon footprint – a credible near-term decarbonisation lever.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
Heineken Ireland captures over 2,000 tonnes of CO2 per year from fermentation and reuses it for beer carbonation and keg pressurisation, achieving 96% CO2 self-sufficiency. [https://www.esmmagazine.com/drinks/heineken-ireland-uses-recycled-carbon-dioxide-to-produce-beer-169159]
Bacardi's distillery captures CO2 from fermentation with plans to supply it to the sparkling-drinks industry, alongside a 50% GHG reduction via biogas and CHP. [https://www.bevnet.com/spirits/2022/bacardi-rum-cuts-greenhouse-gas-emissions-by-50/]

IoT powers remote monitoring, predictive maintenance, and operational optimisation across manufacturing, logistics, and facilities management.
technologies.implications: IoT-based environmental monitoring across all production sites tracks energy consumption, water usage, and waste generation in real time, feeding the group's GHG reporting systems automatically.

PepsiCo uses digital twins and AI modelling to assess carbon impacts of logistics and packaging options across its supply chain. [https://www.klover.ai/pepsico-ai-strategy-analysis-of-dominance-in-consumer-packaged-goods/]

technologies.name: Sustainable Energy Technologies
technologies.description: Technologies for transitioning to clean energy, including solar photovoltaics, wind, energy storage, green hydrogen, and smart grid management. Battery advancements and electrification of industrial processes are key near-term developments. (Stanford Emerging Technology Review 2025)

Diageo's Lebanon, Kentucky distillery runs on 100% renewable electricity, avoiding 117,000 metric tonnes of CO2 annually. [https://www.diageo.com/en/news-and-media/press-releases/2021/diageo-opens-its-first-carbon-neutral-whiskey-distillery-in-north-america]

[https://www.coca-colacompany.com/media-center/the-coca-cola-company-and-microsoft-announce-five-year-strategic-partnership-to-accelerate-cloud-and-generative-ai-initiatives]
Nestle is a member of the Microsoft Frontier Firm AI Initiative and uses an internal GenAI assistant saving employees 45 minutes per week on average. [https://www.hrgrapevine.com/us/content/article/2024-07-23-nestl-says-its-genai-tool-saves-employees-45-minutes-a-week]

technologies.name: Speech recognition
technologies.description: Speech recognition converts spoken language into text or structured commands, enabling voice-controlled interfaces, meeting transcription services, and conversational AI assistants. Advances in deep learning have made modern speech recognition highly accurate across multiple languages, accents, and noisy acoustic environments.
technologies.implications: Real-time speech-to-text and AI meeting transcription ensure knowledge capture from every meeting, making institutional knowledge searchable and reducing the administrative burden on PAs and executive assistants.

technologies.name: Virtual assistant
technologies.description: Virtual assistants are AI-powered conversational agents - delivered via voice or chat interfaces - that understand natural language and perform tasks such as answering queries, scheduling, retrieving information, and triggering workflows on behalf of users. They are increasingly becoming the primary interface between employees and enterprise software in knowledge-intensive organisations.
technologies.implications: Enterprise AI assistants handle scheduling, document retrieval, task prioritisation, and routine approvals autonomously, acting as a force-multiplier for PR's senior management population.

productFamilies.name: Intelligent Workspace
productFamilies.description: Solutions enabling smart offices.

technologies.name: Internet of things
technologies.description: The Internet of Things (IoT) connects physical devices - sensors, machines, and smart objects - to each other and to cloud platforms, enabling real-time data collection and exchange at scale. IoT powers remote monitoring, predictive maintenance, and operational optimisation across manufacturing, logistics, and facilities management.
technologies.implications: IoT occupancy and environmental sensors in PR's offices enable dynamic space allocation, energy optimisation, and a frictionless hybrid work experience – ensuring office space is always optimally configured.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
AccorHotels deployed an IoT room management system at its Singapore property integrating lighting, HVAC, sensors, and occupancy data via a single dashboard. [https://hospitalitytech.com/accorhotel-property-singapore-debuts-iot-room-management-system]
Accor deployed IoT smart room technology across multiple properties including motion sensors, connected tablets controlling all room devices, and AWS-powered AI devices at Mercure London Hyde Park. [https://aws.amazon.com/solutions/case-studies/innovators/accor/]
Heineken connected 3,500 machines to IoT cloud sensors for stock monitoring and beer quality detection across its on-trade assets. [https://robohouse.nl/activities/news/what-a-bottle-picker-can-do/]

technologies.name: Extended Reality (XR)
technologies.description: Extended Reality encompasses VR, AR, and Mixed Reality under a single umbrella. XR headsets create immersive environments for enterprise training, remote collaboration, and consumer entertainment. Tech Opportunity Score: 9/10. (BNP l'atelier Tech Radar – Access / Hardware)
technologies.implications: XR-powered virtual workspaces allow distributed PR teams to collaborate in immersive 3D environments that replicate the spontaneous interaction and shared context of a physical office – critical for global creative teams.
technologies.successfulCasesOtherCompanies:
LVMH / Moet Hennessy deployed VR-powered vine-pruning training simulations (Ampelos) via headsets for workforce skills training at Hennessy. [https://www.lvmh.com/en/news-lvmh/lvmh-takes-viva-technology-2024-visitors-into-its-dream-garden]
L'Oreal uses Microsoft HoloLens environments for client interaction and team member support as part of its extended reality workplace strategy. [https://www.xrtoday.com/mixed-reality/7-xr-companies-on-the-cutting-edge-of-retail/]
Nestle Purina uses Oculus VR headsets for retail shelf planning and employee training. [https://www.industryweek.com/technology-and-iiot/article/55245178/nestle-purinas-robot-dog-pack]

technologies.name: Spatial Computing
technologies.description: Spatial Computing integrates the physical and digital worlds in real time, using sensors, cameras, and AI to overlay and interact with digital content in 3D space. Tech Opportunity Score: 9/10. (BNP l'atelier Tech Radar – Access / Software)
technologies.implications: Spatial computing overlays contextual information (room availability, colleague locations, project status) on office environments through smart glasses or AR apps, reducing friction in the hybrid workplace.
technologies.scientificSupport:
Apple Vision Pro enterprise deployment at SAP and Deloitte — spatial computing for immersive workflow collaboration and 3D data visualisation (2024). [https://www.apple.com/apple-vision-pro/enterprise/]
Meta Quest for Business (Horizon Workrooms) — spatial computing virtual collaboration platform, deployed by Accenture for 60,000 Meta Quest devices (2021–2023). [https://about.meta.com/metaverse/impact/]
